patterns.name: Use spec libraries, don't fall back to manual DOM
patterns.pattern: If the spec calls for a library (e.g. GSAP ScrollTrigger), actually USE it in the implementation. Falling back to manual window.addEventListener('scroll') + getBoundingClientRect() inflates the bundle with dead imports AND violates the spec's architectural intent. Gatekeeper will catch it in review.

patterns.name: Scaffold all sections first, then polish
patterns.pattern: When building a multi-section page, scaffold all sections first so the build passes end-to-end. Then do the polish/animation pass. This prevents getting stuck on one section while others are unbuilt.

patterns.name: CSS-only fallbacks as first class, not afterthoughts
patterns.pattern: Build CSS-only fallbacks for Three.js, fonts, and animations from the start. They're required for mobile, reduced-motion, and SSR. Treating them as afterthoughts means scrambling to add them later.

patterns.name: Real URLs from day 1 â€” no placeholder # hrefs
patterns.pattern: Never ship placeholder # hrefs to Gatekeeper. Nav links, CTA buttons, and footer links should have real URLs from the first commit. Gatekeeper always catches placeholder hrefs. Extra cost: 1 revision round just for link fixes.

patterns.name: Decorative elements must match the design system's emotional register
patterns.pattern: Decorative details (terminal dots, icon colors, etc.) must match the overall emotional register of the design direction. In an 'editorial luxury' design, colored macOS-style terminal dots break the aesthetic even if functionally fine. Every decorative element is a design decision â€” check it against the vibe.

patterns.name: Never hallucinate file operations - verify on disk before claiming done
patterns.pattern: CRITICAL: File operations (copy, resize, write) MUST be followed immediately by Get-ChildItem/Get-Content to verify the result exists on disk. Never claim completion based on what 'should have' happened. Gatekeeper checks actual disk state. Hallucinated deliverables = immediate REJECTED + wasted cycles. If tools fail, say so - don't invent success.

patterns.name: Subagent completion must be manually posted to Slack thread
patterns.pattern: When a subagent (sessions_spawn) completes, OpenClaw auto-routes the result to DM, NOT to the original Slack thread. ALWAYS post the subagent result explicitly using slack-send.ps1 -ReplyTo <threadId>. Never trust auto-routing for thread delivery.

patterns.name: Verify stale tasks before pickup
patterns.pattern: Tasks in queue >24h old may be stale or superseded. Always verify with Xiaosong before picking up old tasks.

patterns.name: No internal reasoning in Slack
patterns.pattern: Before posting ANY Slack message, scan for meta-commentary, decision narration, or self-talk ('let me check...', 'No @mention so...'). If found - do NOT post. Post results only. Violated 3 times. Pre-flight check mandatory. Execute silently: post ONLY the result, never the reasoning.

patterns.name: Syntax-check watchdog.ps1 immediately after every edit
patterns.pattern: CRITICAL: After ANY edit to watchdog.ps1, immediately run: (1) Select-String 'Module 12' to verify the catch block is a single short line, (2) Parse syntax via PowerShell parser, (3) Check file line count is < 1100. A corrupted watchdog silently takes down all monitoring. Catch blocks must use $_ only — never embed multi-line strings or Here-Strings. File size sanity: if > 1100 lines after a small edit, something is wrong.

patterns.name: PS5 hashtable + completion detection in monitoring scripts
patterns.pattern: In PS5: use ConvertTo-Hashtable helper (no -AsHashtable). Completion detection must match emoji-prefixed forms (e.g. ':white_check_mark: DONE'). Self-referential STAGE_MARKERS cause nudge loops - never include output prefixes in trigger list.
